AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS UP TO 105 MPH AND A WIDTH OF ONE HALF TO THREE QUARTERS OF A MILE WIDE TRACKED THREE MILES FROM THREE MILES SOUTH OF WILLOW SPRINGS TO TWO MILES SOUTHEAST OF WILLOW SPRINGS NEAR THE JUNCTION OF HIGHWAYS 60 AND 63. THE TORNADO DOWNED NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER POLES...AND CAUSED MODERATE TO SEVERE DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AND THE HILL BILLY JUNCTION STORE. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM HAS DETERMINED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE SOUTH OF BRIGHTON. THE PATH OF THE TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE LONG AND 200 YARDS WIDE. TWO OUT BUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND AN ADDITIONAL OUT BUILDING AND HOUSE WERE MODERATELY DAMAGED.
ROOF LIFTED OFF HOUSE AND DEBRIS DEPOSITED ON NORTH SIDE OF HIGHWAY 13 1/2 MILE SOUTH OF BRIGHTON. BARN ALSO HEAVILY DAMAGED. GOOSENECK CATTLE TRAILER ALSO DAMAGED. NO INJURIES.

STONE COUNTY OFFICE OF EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT SURVEYED TORNADO DAMAGE APPROXIMATELY 2.5 MILES SOUTH SOUTHEAST OF LAMPE. MINOR DAMAGE CONSISTED OF ONE FRAME HOME...ONE MOBILE HOME...2 LARGE FARM GAS TANKS...AND AN OUTBUILDING.

NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT A STRONG EF-1 TORNADO IMPACTED THE EASTERN SHORE LINE OF THE HIGHWAY DD PENINSULA. NUMEROUS BOAT DOCKS AND BOATS WERE DESTROYED. APPROXIMATELY 60 HOMES EXPERIENCED MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE...WITH ONE WELL BUILT FRAME LOSING ITS ENTIRE ROOF. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 110 MPH.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG THE INDIAN POINT PENINSULA EARLY FRIDAY MORNING. THE TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL BOATS...BOAT DOCKS...AND FRAME HOMES. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 1.75 MILES...WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH. THIS WAS THE SECOND TORNADO TO OCCUR OVER THIS AREA.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 250 YARDS AND WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED NEAR 105 MPH CAUSED MAJOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL STRUCTURES. ONE TRAILER HOME WAS SPUN AND DISPLACED 150 YARDS. A LARGE BARN HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED WITH SEVERAL COLLAPSED WALLS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED WITH MANY OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED.

A NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT A TORNADO IMPACTED THE TABLE ROCK LAKE AREA. SEVERAL BOAT DOCKS AND BOATS WERE EITHER HEAVILY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. THIS SURVEY HAS NOT BEEN COMPLETED AND WILL BE FINISHED SATURDAY. AN EF RATING HAS NOT YET BEEN ASSIGNED TO THIS TORNADO.
EM REPORTS DAMANGE TO A PROPANE TANK AND SEVERAL HOMES IN THE INDIAN POINT AREA. THIS DAMANGE WAS FROM THE FIRST STORM THAT MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.
MOBILE HOME ROLLED OVER INTO JOHNS FORD ROAD...ROOF OFF OF A FRAME HOUSE. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN BLOCKING ROADS IN THE VICINITY. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
TREES DOWN AND A FEW OUTBUILDINGS DAMAGED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BRADEN AND LAROSE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN...DAMAGE TO A FEW BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS...PORCH DAMAGED ON RESIDENCE NEAR THE INTERSTECT OF HIGHWAYS KK AND U. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN AND OUTBUILDINGS DAMAGED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OLGA ROAD AND HIGHWAY U. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. POWER POLES SNAPPED. NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SPOUT SPRINGS ROAD AND DENNY ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
POWER POLES LEANING OVER AT 45 DEGREE ANGLE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SILVER SHADE ROAD AND GREEN HILL ROAD JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY Z. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
THREE TO FOUR FOOT DIAMETER TREE BLOWN DOWN ON A CAR ON GREEN HILL ROAD JUST LESS THAN A MILE EAST OF SPOUT SPRING ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

BASED UPON INITIAL REPORTS FROM THE POLK COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AGENCY...AN NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF0 TORNADO CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO THREE STRUCTURES...INCLUDING TEARING A SECTION OF A ROOF OFF ONE HOME. THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN AT 837 AM NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 155 AND 430TH...5 MILES NORTHEAST OF BOLIVAR AND LIFTED A MINUTE LATER NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 170 AND 420TH...OR ABOUT 6 MILES NORTHEAST OF BOLIVAR.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT EF-3 TORNADO DAMAGE OCCURED OVER FORT LEONARD WOOD MISSOURI. SEVERAL HOMES WERE DESTROYED. ONE HOME HAD ALL WALLS DESTROYED. THE SURVEY IS NOT YET COMPLETE AS MORE DAMAGE MAY HAVE OCCURRED OUTSIDE OF THE BASE.

*** 1 FATAL, 6 INJ *** EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED 1 CONFIRMED FATALITY AND 6 INJURIES ALONG WITH EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO MOBILE HOMES AND STRUCTURES ALONG COUNTY ROAD 8040. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE...NWS PERSONEL ARE CURRENTLY IN ROUTE TO COMPLETE AN OFFICIAL DAMAGE SURVEY.

*** 2 FATAL *** LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS 3 MOBILE HOMES DESTROYED AND EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO NUMEROUS STRUCTURES ALONG COUNTY ROAD 2050.
